context:
<Profile>### IDENTITY
- **Name:** Jessica Kim  
- **Age:** 29  
- **Gender:** female  
- **Experience level:** **Intermediate** — 2 years consistent CrossFit + 3 years general fitness prior; competed Scaled at 3 local throwdowns (top 10 twice); first bar muscle-up in fall 2024.

---

### GOALS
- **Primary goal(s):** **Performance (CrossFit → Rx readiness)** for **2025 CrossFit Open (8 weeks away, as of 2026-02-27)**  
  - String **bar muscle-ups** (currently can perform singles; first achieved fall 2024)  
  - Increase Olympic lifts:  
    - **Clean & Jerk:** **155 → 175 lb**  
    - **Snatch:** **115 → 135 lb**  
  - Improve “engine” for **longer chippers** (sustained mixed-modal conditioning)
- **Secondary goals:** Maintain consistency at **5 sessions/week**; manage wrist to tolerate higher-volume overhead/Oly work.
- **Target events/deadlines:** **2025 CrossFit Open — ~8 weeks out** *(date not provided; tracked relative to today)*

---

## TRAINING CONTEXT

### Schedule & Availability
- **Available training days:** **Mon / Tue / Wed / Fri / Sat**
- **Desired training frequency:** **5x/week**
- **Time window:** **5:30 PM class** (on training days)
- **Blocked days:** **Thu, Sun** (reason not specified)

### Equipment & Environment
- **Training location:** Commercial gym — **CrossFit Riverdale affiliate**
- **Equipment available (full CF setup):**
  - Barbells + bumper plates
  - Pull-up rigs / **pull-up bars**, **rings**
  - Rowers, **Assault bikes**
  - Rope climbs, plyo boxes, GHD
  - Kettlebells **8–70 lb**
  - Dumbbells **10–100 lb**

### Constraints
- **[ACTIVE] (since 2024-03-01, month approximate)** Right wrist strain history from high-rep overhead work  
  - **Management:** mobility + taping  
  - **Triggers:** flares with **>30 snatches** or **>50 HSPU** in a single session  
  - **Programming note:** monitor total overhead volume/intensity; prefer distributing snatch/HSPU volume across sessions when possible.

### Preferences
- **Training style:** CrossFit class-based training (group class at set time)
- **Focus areas:** bar muscle-up consistency/volume tolerance; Olympic lifting strength + technical efficiency; longer mixed-modal pacing
- **Not stated yet:** movement dislikes, communication style preferences

---

## METRICS
*(CrossFit performance prioritized; current numbers need “as of” dates—only provided contextually.)*

### Strength / Skill (CrossFit-relevant)
- **Bar Muscle-up:** first achieved **fall 2024** (currently not yet stringing; capacity unclear)  
- **Clean & Jerk:** **155 lb → goal 175 lb** *(current lift date not provided; as of 2026-02-27, date needed for validation)*  
- **Snatch:** **115 lb → goal 135 lb** *(current lift date not provided; as of 2026-02-27, date needed for validation)*

### Endurance (Engine)
- **Long chipper capacity:** improvement goal stated; **no baseline metrics provided** (e.g., row/bike paces, benchmark WOD times, HR data)

### Movement Quality / Tolerance
- **Wrist overhead volume tolerance constraint:** avoid/modify sessions exceeding **30 snatches** or **50 HSPU** (per-session threshold)

### Body Composition
- **Not provided**

---

## LOG

## 2026-02-27 — Profile created/updated (Open prep + Rx transition)
- Confirmed athlete background: 2 years CrossFit; prior general fitness 3 years; 3 scaled throwdowns (top 10 twice).
- Set primary performance goal: transition **Scaled → Rx**; prepare for **2025 CrossFit Open (~8 weeks out)**.
- Captured skill/strength targets: string bar muscle-ups; **C&J 155→175**, **Snatch 115→135**.
- Captured schedule: **Mon/Tue/Wed/Fri/Sat 5:30 PM class**, target **5x/week**.
- Logged **[ACTIVE]** right wrist strain history; identified flare thresholds and management strategy.

---

### Validation notes (data consistency checks)
- “**2025 CrossFit Open (8 weeks away)**” is **inconsistent with today’s date (2026-02-27)** unless the year is a typo or the date reference is historical.  
  - To fix: confirm whether you mean **2026 Open** (or provide the exact start date of the Open you’re targeting).
- Current lift numbers (**155 C&J, 115 Snatch**) need **dates** to be fully valid “as of” metrics.

If you reply with (1) the correct Open year/date, (2) your best recent lift dates (and whether they’re 1RM or a rep max), and (3) one or two benchmark conditioning results (e.g., Fran, Grace, 2k row, 10-min bike cals), I’ll tighten the METRICS section and make the timeline fully consistent.</Profile>

reference.response:
## Header
- **Program name:** Open-Ready Rx Build (8-Week CrossFit Performance Peak)
- **Program Owner:** GymText
- **User:** Jessica Kim
- **Goal:** CrossFit → **Rx readiness** for the Open (8 weeks out), with emphasis on **stringing bar muscle-ups**, improving **C&J (155→175)** and **Snatch (115→135)**, and building **long chipper engine**
- **Duration:** **8 weeks** (Open prep block)

---

## Program Philosophy
Jessica is an intermediate CrossFit athlete with solid consistency, recent bar muscle-up breakthrough, and clear performance targets tied to the Open. This program uses a **5-day structure (Mon/Tue/Wed/Fri/Sat)** that mirrors typical affiliate training rhythm while layering in **short, high-impact skill + strength progressions** to drive measurable gains without requiring extra training days. The plan prioritizes **(1) bar muscle-up density/quality**, **(2) Olympic lift efficiency + heavy exposure**, and **(3) mixed-modal aerobic power for chippers**, while explicitly managing her **right wrist** by **distributing overhead volume** and avoiding single-session spikes (notably **>30 snatches** or **>50 HSPU**). Progression is **hybrid (RPE + % guidelines)**: early weeks build volume/positions and repeatable skill, mid-block increases intensity and Open-style density, and the final phase tapers fatigue while keeping speed and confidence high.

---

# Phase 1 (Weeks 1–3): Foundation + Skill Density (Volume, Positions, Aerobic Base)

### Weekly Pattern

**Mon — Bar MU Skill + Snatch Technique + Aerobic Intervals**  
- **Focus:** bar MU efficiency + snatch positions; aerobic power without wrist flare  
- **Volume:** ~14–18 working sets + 12–20 min conditioning

- **Bar Muscle-up Skill (EMOM 10):** 1 rep (or 1 attempt) @ **RPE 6–7**  
  - Scale options: jumping bar MU + slow negative, banded bar MU, or 2–3 chest-to-bar + 1 fast turnover drill  
  - *Builds “on-the-minute” composure and repeatability without trash volume.*
- **Tall Snatch + Overhead Squat:** 4 × (2 + 2) @ **RPE 6–7**  
  - *Reinforces vertical pull + stable catch with lower loading and lower rep count to respect wrist.*
- **Snatch Pull (pause at knee):** 3 × 3 @ **RPE 7**
- **Conditioning (choose row/bike to reduce wrist load):**  
  - **5 rounds:** 2:00 hard (RPE 8) / 1:00 easy

**Tue — Clean & Jerk Strength + Mixed-Modal Threshold**  
- **Focus:** heavier C&J exposure; “Open-style” pacing at sustainable discomfort  
- **Volume:** ~12–16 working sets + 10–14 min metcon

- **Clean & Jerk (tech → heavy):** 6 × 1 @ **RPE 7** (clean reps must be fast; stop if grindy)  
- **Front Squat:** 4 × 4 @ **RPE 7–8**  
  - *Direct carryover to clean recovery and confidence under fatigue.*
- **Jerk Dip Squat (from rack):** 3 × 5 @ **RPE 7**  
  - *Leg drive + dip/drive consistency with minimal wrist cycling.*
- **Metcon (12 min):** AMRAP  
  - 12/10 cal bike  
  - 10 toes-to-bar  
  - 8 DB front rack lunges (moderate)  
  - *Chipper-relevant trunk + leg stamina without huge overhead reps.*

**Wed — Gymnastics Capacity + Upper Strength (Wrist-smart) + Easy Flush**  
- **Focus:** BMU capacity accessories; pulling strength; recovery-biased conditioning  
- **Volume:** ~16–20 working sets + 12–20 min easy

- **BMU Turnover + Push/Pull Superset (quality):** 4 rounds  
  - Bar transition drill (feet-on-box) 5 reps @ controlled tempo  
  - Strict chest-to-bar pull-ups 5–8 @ RPE 7  
  - *Builds the “around-the-bar” skill + pulling strength that makes stringing possible.*
- **Strict HSPU / Pike HSPU:** 4 × 6–10 @ **RPE 7**  
  - Keep total weekly HSPU volume managed; **do not chase failure**  
- **Ring Rows (tempo 31X1):** 3 × 10–12 @ RPE 7  
- **Easy engine:** 15–20 min Zone 2 (bike/row)  
  - *Supports chipper stamina and recovery.*

**Fri — Snatch + Open-Style Mixed Modal (Short-to-Mid)**
- **Focus:** snatch speed under light fatigue; faster Open cycling practice without high rep totals  
- **Volume:** ~12–16 working sets + 8–12 min metcon

- **Power Snatch + Overhead Squat:** 5 × (1 + 1) @ **RPE 7**  
- **Snatch Balance (light/moderate):** 4 × 2 @ **RPE 6–7**  
  - *Confidence receiving + footwork without lots of total snatch reps.*
- **Metcon (10 min):**  
  - 10 wall balls  
  - 10 box jump overs  
  - 8 pull-ups / C2B  
  - 6 burpees over DB  
  - *Keeps intensity high; wrist impact low.*

**Sat — Long Chipper Builder (Pacing + Transitions)**  
- **Focus:** sustained mixed-modal capacity; fueling/pacing practice  
- **Volume:** 1 long piece (20–35 min) + short accessory

- **Chipper (Week 1–3 progression):** 20–35 min cap, steady-hard (RPE 7–8)  
  - Example template (adjust weekly):  
    - 1,000m row  
    - 50 KB swings (moderate; American only if wrist tolerates—otherwise Russian)  
    - 40 goblet squats  
    - 30 toes-to-bar / knee raises  
    - 20 DB step-overs  
    - 1,000m row  
- **Accessory (optional, 8–10 min):** posterior chain + trunk (back extensions, dead bug, side plank)  
  - *Keeps you durable for high Open volume.*

#### Phase 1 Progression Strategy (Weeks 1–3)
- **Bar MU EMOM:** keep EMOM structure; progress by **adding a 2nd rep every other minute** *only if* all reps stay crisp (no chicken-wing, no long press-out). Otherwise stay at 1 rep and improve speed.
- **Snatch/C&J:** add load weekly **only if bar speed stays high** (target RPE 7–8 top work). If wrist is irritated, keep load and reduce overhead extras first (e.g., drop snatch balance volume).
- **Chipper Saturdays:** increase duration slightly each week (e.g., ~22 → 28 → 32 minutes) or add reps, but keep intensity at “could negative split.”

---

# Phase 2 (Weeks 4–6): Build + Open Specificity (Higher Intensity, More Repeatable Metcons)

> **Week 4 is a deload pivot inside this phase** (reduced volume, maintain intensity touches).

### Weekly Pattern

**Mon — Bar MU Volume Ladder + Snatch Heavy Single Exposure**
- **Focus:** turning singles into small sets; snatch confidence  
- **Volume:** ~12–16 working sets + short finisher

- **Bar MU Density (10 min):** accumulate **8–15 total reps** (singles or 1–2s) @ RPE 7–8  
  - Rule: stop a set if rep speed or turnover quality drops
- **Snatch:** build to **1 heavy single @ RPE 8**, then 3 × 1 @ 90% of that  
  - Keep **total snatch reps ≤ 25–30/session** (wrist threshold)
- **Finisher (8 min):** bike sprint intervals or row intervals (wrist-light)

**Tue — C&J Heavy + Front Squat + Short Open Burner**
- **Focus:** C&J top-end strength; fast, repeatable discomfort  
- **Volume:** ~14–18 working sets + 6–10 min metcon

- **Clean & Jerk:** 7 × 1 @ **RPE 8** (last 1–2 singles may be near RPE 8.5 if sharp)  
- **Front Squat:** 5 × 3 @ **RPE 8**  
- **Metcon (8–10 min):** Assault bike cals + light barbell cycling (choose movement that doesn’t spike snatches)

**Wed — Gymnastics + Upper Pull + Zone 2**
- **Focus:** strict strength to support BMU strings; recovery  
- **Volume:** ~16–22 working sets

- **Strict Pull-ups or Weighted Pull-ups:** 5 × 3–6 @ RPE 8  
- **BMU Skill (tech, low fatigue):** 10 min of drills (transition + tap swing timing)  
- **Handstand walking / wall-facing holds (wrist dependent):** 6–10 min skill  
  - If wrist flares: substitute **sled push** conditioning + core
- **Zone 2:** 15–25 min easy

**Fri — Open Simulation (Mid-Length) + Overhead Volume Management**
- **Focus:** pacing strategy, transitions, movement standards  
- **Volume:** 1 main piece + 2–4 accessories

- **Open-style piece (12–18 min):** rotate weekly between  
  - mixed-modal AMRAP,  
  - couplet/triplet with gymnastics + monostructural,  
  - or barbell + bodyweight (avoid snatch totals >30)  
- **Accessory (choose 2):**  
  - Single-leg strength (rear-foot elevated split squat) 3 × 8/side @ RPE 7  
  - Midline (GHD sit-ups or hollow rocks) 3–4 sets  
  - Scap/shoulder health (banded external rotations, YTWs) 2–3 sets  
  - *Keeps performance high without adding risky overhead volume.*

**Sat — Long Chipper (More “Open Feel”)**
- **Focus:** sustained output + grip management + strategy  
- **Volume:** 25–40 min primary work

- **Chipper:** 1 big piece weekly, aim for slightly higher intensity than Phase 1 (RPE 8)  
  - Include at least 1–2 of: rowing/bike, wall balls, TTB, burpees, light/moderate barbell cycling  
  - Keep overhead spikes controlled (split overhead work across days)

#### Phase 2 Progression Strategy (Weeks 4–6)
- **Week 4 deload (mandatory):**
  - Reduce working sets ~40–50% across strength/skill
  - Keep 1–2 heavy-ish singles at **RPE 7–8** for Oly lifts (no grinders)
  - Conditioning stays but at **RPE 6–7**, shorter duration
- **Weeks 5–6 build:**
  - **C&J**: increase top singles gradually (RPE 8 → 8.5), prioritize successful makes
  - **Snatch**: 1 heavy single weekly + back-off singles; never chase misses repeatedly
  - **BMU**: aim to progress from “total reps” → **small sets (2s)**. A good target by end of Week 6 is **3–5 sets of 2** across a session, resting as needed.

---

# Phase 3 (Weeks 7–8): Peak + Taper (Open Readiness, Speed, Confidence)

### Weekly Pattern

**Mon — BMU Sharpness + Snatch Speed**
- **Focus:** crisp reps, low fatigue  
- **Volume:** ~10–14 working sets

- **BMU:** EMOM 8–10: 1–2 reps (only if perfect)  
- **Snatch:** 6–8 × 1 @ **75–85%** (RPE 6–7, fast)  
  - *Rehearses speed without wrist-irritating volume.*

**Tue — C&J Heavy Touch + Front Squat Light**
- **Focus:** keep heavy feel; minimal soreness  
- **Volume:** ~10–12 working sets

- **Clean & Jerk:** build to **1–2 singles @ RPE 8–9** (no misses if possible)  
- **Front Squat:** 3 × 2 @ RPE 7

**Wed — Short Open Primer**
- **Focus:** breathing + transitions + standards  
- **Volume:** 8–12 min main + small accessories

- **Primer WOD:** 6–10 minutes at RPE 8 (stop before a death spiral)  
- **Mobility + wrist care:** 10–15 minutes (see note below)

**Fri — Pre-Open Shakeout (Very Light)**
- **Focus:** feel good, move well  
- **Volume:** 30–45 min easy total

- 10 min easy machine  
- 10 min movement flow (air squat, hinge, scap pull-ups, light empty bar)  
- 2–3 short practice bursts (10–20 sec) at Open intensity, full recovery

**Sat — Open / Mock Open**
- **Focus:** performance  
- If an Open workout is released: do it with full warm-up and standards  
- If not: run a **mock Open** (10–16 min) emphasizing likely movements (gymnastics + monostructural + light/mod barbell)

#### Phase 3 Progression Strategy (Weeks 7–8)
- **Week 7:** keep intensity touches, reduce total volume ~20–30% from Phase 2.
- **Week 8 (taper):** reduce volume ~40–60%, keep **short intensity** to stay sharp.
- Goal is to arrive with **high nervous system readiness** and **low wrist irritation**—you should finish most sessions feeling like you could do more.

---

## Wrist Management Rules (Applies All Phases)
- **Hard cap per session (guideline):**
  - **Snatches:** stay **≤ 30 total reps** (count warm-ups + work sets)  
  - **HSPU:** stay **≤ 50 total reps** (including kipping volume)
- Prefer **distributed overhead**: snatch technique Monday + heavy/speed exposures Friday, rather than a single huge overhead day.
- Use **wrist wrap/tape** for high-rep days; emphasize **front rack mobility** and **thoracic extension** to reduce compensations.
- If wrist pain climbs above “mild awareness,” modify immediately:
  - Replace high-rep overhead with **rowing/bike + lower-body/trunk** work
  - Swap HSPU for **pike HSPU**, **strict press**, or **hand-release push-ups** depending on tolerance

---

## Phase Cycling
This is an **8-week event-focused block** that runs:
1. **Foundation + Skill Density (Weeks 1–3)** → build repeatable BMU and Oly positions, expand aerobic base  
2. **Build + Open Specificity (Weeks 4–6)** → higher intensity, more Open-like workouts; Week 4 deload to absorb gains  
3. **Peak + Taper (Weeks 7–8)** → maintain intensity, reduce fatigue, maximize performance readiness

**After the Open:** recycle into a new 8–12 week cycle based on outcomes:
- If BMU remains a limiter → dedicate a 6-week gymnastics capacity block
- If Oly totals stall → run an Oly-focused strength/tech block with lower metcon density
- If chippers remain the limiter → shift to aerobic volume + tempo intervals while maintaining strength

---

## Modification History
- **2026-02-27:** Initial program created for **8-week Open prep**, built around **Mon/Tue/Wed/Fri/Sat schedule**, with explicit **wrist overhead-volume caps** and dedicated **BMU + Oly + chipper** progression.

--- 

If you confirm (1) the exact Open start date/year, (2) whether 155/115 are true 1RMs or “best recent,” and (3) one conditioning benchmark (e.g., 2k row, Fran/Grace time, 10-min bike cals), I can tighten Phase 2–3 loading targets and make the weekly “mock Open” prescriptions more precise.
